message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first action-plan persist in workflow runner (tick 259)

Migrates the action-plan persistence site inside
`WorkflowRunner::run_workflow_inner` to use the maybe-cx dispatcher
pattern already used for adjacent `storage.get_workflow` /
`storage.get_pane` calls in the same function (runner.rs L629-L636,
L643-L650).

The fn takes `cx: Option<&Cx>`. Prior to this tick, the action-plan
persist at L713 was unconditionally ambient:

    if let Err(e) = self.storage.upsert_action_plan(execution_id, &plan).await { ... }

Now dispatches on the cx-availability:

    #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
    let persist_result = if let Some(cx) = cx {
        self.storage.upsert_action_plan_with_cx(cx, execution_id, &plan).await
    } else {
        self.storage.upsert_action_plan(execution_id, &plan).await
    };
    #[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]
    let persist_result = self.storage.upsert_action_plan(execution_id, &plan).await;
    if let Err(e) = persist_result { ... }

Under asupersync-runtime, any caller of `run_workflow_inner` that
threads a `Some(cx)` — which includes the ft-xbnl0.2.3 cx-first entry
points (`run_workflow_cx`, `handle_detection_with_cx`) — now propagates
cancel into the action-plan write. Ambient callers (legacy paths)
retain the bare upsert to preserve the legacy path contract.

This is the maybe-cx fallback dispatcher pattern (same as the tick-205-
era wait.rs / retry.rs seams) applied to one additional runner.rs site
that was missed in earlier sweeps.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 73 (187-259).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 32 (runtime 7 + mcp 2 + tui 8 + web 4
+ mcp_helpers 2 + mcp_tools 8 + workflows/runner 1).
main.rs cross-crate total: 127 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
